key_insight: MTP rollout speed in RL is limited by entropy growth, but rejection sampling with an end-to-end TV loss can recover high acceptance and meaningful training acceleration.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path to speed up large-scale RL post-training pipelines for LLMs without costly online MTP updates.
method: Analyze how entropy fluctuations bound MTP acceptance during RL training.
Use probabilistic rejection sampling instead of greedy draft sampling to reduce entropy-induced degradation.
Train with an end-to-end TV loss that directly optimizes multi-step acceptance rate.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports about 10% higher acceptance rates, up to 95% acceptance, up to 25% extra inference throughput, and up to 1.8x end-to-end async RL acceleration across multiple Qwen models.
watch_item: The gains depend on maintaining stable entropy behavior and integrating rejection sampling carefully into the RL rollout pipeline.
paper_title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
